findings.issue: readScreenWithHaiku uses claude-haiku model but extractInsightsWithOpus also uses haiku instead of opus, defeating the function name contract and wasting tokens on wrong model.
findings.suggestion: Change line 96 model to 'claude-opus-4-1-20250805' for actual insight extraction capability.
findings.est_impact: 60% token savings by using correct model tier; opus better suited for complex trading signal extraction.

findings.issue: Every scrapeChannel call makes 3 screenshots and processes each with two separate API calls (haiku + haiku), creating redundant LLM processing.
findings.suggestion: Combine rawText extraction and insights into single API call with vision + analysis prompt to halve token usage per channel.
findings.est_impact: 50% reduction in tokens per channel (3 channels × 2 calls = 6 calls → 3 calls).

findings.issue: No validation that runPython() succeeded before proceeding; openUrl/scrollDown/takeScreenshot all silently fail if desktop.py is unavailable.
findings.suggestion: Check runPython return value in openUrl/scrollDown; throw error if null instead of continuing with stale screenshots.
findings.est_impact: Prevents silent data corruption from failed screenshots being treated as valid content.

findings.issue: SCREENSHOT_PATH is written to disk but never read back; only base64 is used.
findings.suggestion: Remove fs.writeFileSync() call on line 62 to eliminate unnecessary disk I/O.
findings.est_impact: Slight performance improvement; PNG persists unnecessarily.
